author.name: Bran
author.bio: NYC - founder/Physicist

project.name: Loyalty-Test.com - Partner Loyalty Testing Marketplace
project.summary: A two-sided marketplace where users can hire 'testers' to DM and flirt with their significant other to test loyalty. The site is live at loyalty-test.com, features real tester profiles, and has been covered by the New York Times, Business Insider, and Barstool Sports. The founder claims $200k+ in revenue built solo.

enrichment.write_up: Bran is a NYC-based physicist-turned-founder who built Loyalty-Test.com solo — a two-sided marketplace where people hire strangers to flirt with their partners — and somehow turned it into a $200k+ revenue business that landed in the NYT, Business Insider, and Barstool Sports. It's a weird idea executed with real distribution instincts: live product, real tester profiles, actual press, actual money. Small follower count, zero hype machine — just a builder who found a market and ran with it.

author.name: Sriram Kiron
author.bio: Building @MoltopiaOrg, a virtual world for OpenClaw agents, & https://t.co/xxHO5Ou6RC, a way to speed up videos. @PurdueCS, @purduehackers, & Codex Ambassador.

project.name: Moltopia - Virtual World for AI Agents
project.summary: Moltopia (moltopia.org) is a live, functioning virtual world populated by autonomous OpenClaw AI agents that can chat, craft, trade, negotiate loans, and form alliances. The screenshot clearly shows a pixel-art game world with 6 agents online, named characters (Finn, Lux, Drift, Kimi, Nova, Ember) actively performing tasks, and a real-time activity feed — demonstrating a working, deployed product with genuine complexity.

enrichment.write_up: Purdue CS student who built Moltopia — a live pixel-art virtual world where autonomous AI agents chat, trade, craft, and negotiate loans with each other in real time. This isn't a demo or mockup; it's a deployed product with named agents actively running an economy. Also shipping a video acceleration tool on the side, which tells you he doesn't wait around.

author.name: ultravioleta
author.bio: (PART 2) learning in public about web4, building @UltravioletaDAO @executi0nmarket | prev: @cuchorapido

project.name: Execution Market + MeshRelay — AI Agent Task & Communication Infrastructure
project.summary: Execution Market (execution.market) is a live platform where AI agents post real-world tasks (photo verification, location checks) paid instantly in stablecoins across 10 blockchain networks, with 57 registered users and 252 completed tasks showing real traction. MeshRelay (meshrelay.xyz) is a companion IRC-based agent communication network with x402 payments, ERC-8004 on-chain identity, and an OpenAPI, showing live agents and message activity. Both projects are deployed and functional with real usage metrics.

enrichment.write_up: Ultravioleta is building live, working AI agent infrastructure — not whitepapers. Execution Market already has 57 users and 252 completed tasks where AI agents post real-world jobs (photo verification, location checks) and pay out instantly in stablecoins across 10 chains; MeshRelay adds an IRC-based agent comms layer with on-chain identity and x402 payments. This is someone who ships functional systems in a space full of vaporware, learning loudly and moving fast.

project.name: Three Products: SimpleMedDocs, SportsCal, Uno Link
project.summary: The author built three live, deployed web products: SimpleMedDocs (simplemeddocs.com) translates hospital discharge paperwork into plain English with medication schedules and calendar reminders; SportsCal (sportscal.io) auto-syncs sports team schedules to Google/Apple/Outlook calendars with watch links and schedule updates; and Uno Link (uno-link.com) creates a single shareable link that opens up to 10 URLs simultaneously with a $9/year pro plan. All three sites are live and functional with clear product descriptions, pricing, and working features.

enrichment.write_up: Zlatko is a self-taught builder who ships — three live, revenue-generating products across healthcare, sports, and productivity, all deployed and functional. SimpleMedDocs alone shows real product instinct: turning dense hospital discharge paperwork into plain English with medication schedules is a genuinely useful, underserved problem. Low follower count, high output — the kind of quiet executor who lets the work speak.

author.name: Ozair Khan
author.bio: What are we doing here

project.name: smash.watch – Esports Analytics Platform
project.summary: A live web platform that analyzes competitive Super Smash Bros. bracket data to rank players by weighted win rate and opponent strength at the state level in the US. Features an interactive scatter plot dashboard, filter controls, and documentation. Has a GitHub repo and is deployed at smash.watch.

enrichment.write_up: Ozair built smash.watch, a live esports analytics platform that pulls competitive Super Smash Bros. bracket data and ranks players using weighted win rates and opponent strength — the kind of nuanced, domain-specific metric design that most data people skip over. He shipped it end-to-end: custom ranking algorithm, interactive scatter plot dashboard, filter controls, docs, and a live deployment at his own domain. Small following, zero fluff — just someone who picked a hard problem in a niche space and actually finished it.

They also solo-built a DeFi Earn iOS app using Swift, TypeScript, Passkeys, and Morpho, and have multiple ETHGlobal hackathon wins. The SDK repo has real commits, documentation, and a meaningful readme.

enrichment.write_up: Jan is a DeFi engineer who shipped real infrastructure at Index Coop — the FlashMint SDK is live on npm, open source, and still being actively maintained into 2026. He solo-built a DeFi Earn iOS app in Swift with Passkeys and Morpho integration, and has stacked multiple ETHGlobal hackathon wins. The kind of full-stack crypto builder who goes from protocol-level SDK to consumer iOS app without blinking.

Has the full stack range to go from React + Three.js 3D environments to Solana key management with Privy, and clearly doesn't wait for permission to build. Crypto-native background with a builder's bias toward action — the kind of person who treats a hiring tweet as a starting gun.

project.summary: A solo-developed mobile app that reached 4,000 users, 350 paid subscriptions, and $3,500 MRR within 4 months of release. The app is live at skanujemy.app (returning HTTP 429, indicating real traffic/load). The author brings a unique background of 4 years professional poker and 5 years software development.

enrichment.write_up: Maciek built Skanujemy.app solo and took it from zero to 4,000 users and $3,500 MRR in four months — that's a builder who ships and converts. His background is unusual in the best way: 4 years of professional poker before pivoting into 5 years of software development, meaning he understands risk, probability, and reading situations under pressure. No Twitter presence or follower count to speak of, but the product does the talking.

author.name: jimmy valentine
author.bio: pseudo-intellectual creature of leisure

project.name: AnthroVision – Body Measurement Scanner
project.summary: Built a web-based body scanning service called AnthroVision that extracts 24 body measurements (accurate to 1cm) in under 30 seconds from a user-submitted video of themselves spinning in an A-pose. Also built a Telegram bot (Claw plugin) integration so videos can be sent through Telegram. The live site is accessible at body-measurement-scanner.vercel.app.

enrichment.write_up: Jimmy built AnthroVision, a web-based body scanner that pulls 24 precise measurements from a short spin video in under 30 seconds — the kind of computer vision + UX compression that's genuinely hard to pull off. He shipped it to a live URL and bolted on a Telegram bot integration, which shows he thinks about real distribution channels, not just demos. The 'pseudo-intellectual creature of leisure' bio is a red flag for nothing except maybe too much range.

author.name: flamy.gg
author.bio: Building the first @predictdotfun terminal, discord:  https://t.co/OGlbAHAiNl

project.name: flamy.gg - predict.fun Terminal
project.summary: Flamy.gg is a real-time analytics terminal for the predict.fun prediction markets platform, offering whale wallet tracking, live trade charts, oracle monitoring (UMA), and market intelligence. The site is live, claims to track $10M+ daily volume across thousands of markets, with sub-1 second latency and 5-second whale alerts. It's free to use with no account required.

enrichment.write_up: Flamy built the first third-party terminal for predict.fun from scratch — real-time whale tracking, oracle monitoring, live trade charts, sub-1 second latency across $10M+ daily volume. No login, no paywall, just shipped. Small following but the product speaks louder — this is someone who identifies a gap in a fast-moving market and fills it before anyone else does.

project.name: GrepJob
project.summary: A job board for software engineers that scrapes company career pages every hour. Generates $350/month in revenue with 2,000 monthly active users. The site returned HTTP 429 (rate limited, not dead), and the author reports concrete traction including a friend getting an interview through the platform.

enrichment.write_up: Kyle just made his first dollar online in April 2025 and already has $350/month MRR and 2,000 MAUs — that's a real product, not a side project. GrepJob scrapes company career pages hourly to surface software engineering jobs, and it's already gotten someone an interview, which is the only metric that matters for a job board. He's early, hungry, and building in public with concrete goals — exactly the kind of scrappy operator Ramp should want to talk to before he hits $500 MRR and stops returning emails.

author.name: vik
author.bio: Reading Quantum/ML research papers

project.name: Zcrafter — Natural Language Interface for Mainframe Computers
project.summary: Zcrafter is a chat-based AI platform (zcrafter.com) that allows users to interact with IBM mainframe computers via natural language. The screenshot shows a polished dark-themed UI connected to a live mainframe endpoint (204.90.115.200) with specific mainframe actions like Browse Datasets, Submit JCL, Job Output, USS Files, and Patch Code. A video demo is also attached to the tweet.

enrichment.write_up: Vik built Zcrafter — a natural language interface that lets you talk to IBM mainframes, arguably one of the least approachable pieces of enterprise infrastructure on the planet. The demo shows a live, polished dark-themed UI hitting a real mainframe endpoint with actual ops: browsing datasets, submitting JCL, pulling job output. Someone who's reading quantum/ML papers for fun and shipping AI wrappers on 50-year-old mainframe tech is exactly the kind of technically fearless builder Ramp should talk to.

On the side, they built a jet plane scanner for kids on Yoto players that won the platform's developer challenge and now reaches 30,000 families monthly — that's not a weekend hack, that's a real product with real scale. The combo of music industry depth, consumer product instincts, and the ability to ship something kids actually use speaks for itself.

project.summary: An AI phone agent built for plumbers that answers inbound calls, captures urgency, qualifies leads, and sends structured SMS summaries post-call. enrichment.write_up: Manu is a PM in healthcare fintech who got tired of just writing specs and built CallPilot — a voice AI agent for plumbers that handles inbound calls, triages urgency, and fires off structured SMS summaries post-call. Self-taught on the technical side, he's shipping real infrastructure (voice + dispatch handoffs) in a unglamorous but cash-heavy vertical while holding down a full-time job. The combo of product instincts, domain hustle, and DIY technical execution is exactly the profile worth a conversation.

project.name: Local-first AI Excel Control (Tauri App)
project.summary: A Tauri-based desktop application that allows users to control Excel through natural language commands, running locally. A video thumbnail shows the app overlaid on an actual Excel spreadsheet with a floating toolbar UI. The project demonstrates a working integration between an AI interface and Excel.

enrichment.write_up: Saul built a local-first, Tauri-based desktop app that lets you control Excel through natural language — no cloud, no API leakage, just a floating UI sitting on top of your spreadsheet. That's a legitimately hard integration (Tauri + Excel COM/automation + LLM) and he shipped something visual and working. Thin social footprint, but the project speaks louder than a follower count.

project.name: Permify - Open Source Fine-Grained Authorization
project.summary: Permify is an open-source authorization-as-a-service inspired by Google Zanzibar, built in Go, enabling fine-grained and scalable access control systems. It has 5,833 GitHub stars, covers RBAC/ABAC/ReBAC patterns, and was successfully acquired by FusionAuth. The project represents a full build-fundraise-exit cycle with clear technical substance.

enrichment.write_up: Fred co-founded Permify, an open-source Google Zanzibar-inspired authorization engine built in Go that hit 5,800+ GitHub stars and got acquired by FusionAuth — a full build-to-exit arc with real technical depth. He's lived the zero-to-acquisition grind on a hard infrastructure problem (fine-grained access control across RBAC/ABAC/ReBAC) that most engineers never touch. The kind of founder-engineer who ships things people actually use and finds a buyer for it.

project.name: Cred Ninja (OAuth2 credential delegation SDK for AI agents) + Research-Compiler pipeline
project.summary: Cred Ninja is a TypeScript SDK providing OAuth2 credential delegation middleware for AI agents, supporting 7 providers (Google, GitHub, Slack, Notion, Salesforce, Linear, HubSpot) with an encrypted local vault, published to npm. The Research-Compiler is a Python autonomous pipeline that takes a vague idea through research → lowering pass → factory compiler to produce runnable tickets and bootstrap scripts for coding agents. Both repos have real code, READMEs, and recent commit activity.

project.name: Clira - Open-source AI Chief of Staff

The GitHub repo was created in February 2026, last updated March 2026, has 12 stars, comprehensive documentation (self-host guide, architecture docs, contributor setup), Docker support, CI/CD pipeline, and integrations with Telegram, WhatsApp, PostgreSQL, and MCP.
